name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
description: The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable or unintended results. In languages like C and C++, stack variables contain junk data by default, potentially allowing attackers to read sensitive memory contents or influence control flow.
detection_pattern: STORAGE node (variable) flows to a TRANSFORM, CONTROL, or EGRESS node without a preceding TRANSFORM(assignment) node. In memory-unsafe languages, the STORAGE node may contain residual data from previous operations, creating paths to denial of service, information disclosure, or code execution.
